channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
item.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.content:encoded:
Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

(Image credit: Allison Lee Isley)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: If a Lyme disease vaccine gets approved, how would it go over? We asked hunters
item.description: Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?
item.content:encoded:
Drugmakers are working on a potential new shot to prevent the tick-borne illness. How might it fare in the era of vaccine skepticism?

(Image credit: Kyle Pyatt for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith

item.title: 5 million have dropped ACA insurance after Trump and the GOP let prices skyrocket
item.description: The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.
item.content:encoded:
The number of people who signed up for a health plan and then didn't pay their premium fell much more sharply than it has in years past. Trump officials blame fraud. Health policy experts blame costs.

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised: "We have a whole-of-government response. It'll be big; it'll be fast; and it'll be effective."
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised: "We have a whole-of-government response. It'll be big; it'll be fast; and it'll be effective."

(Image credit: Federico Parra/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Supreme Court backs Monsanto in its fight against liability from popular weed killer
item.description: The central issue in the Roundup case, filed by Missouri resident John Durnell, was who decides what should appear on a pesticide or insecticide label and whether a federal law overrides state claims.
item.content:encoded:
The central issue in the Roundup case, filed by Missouri resident John Durnell, was who decides what should appear on a pesticide or insecticide label and whether a federal law overrides state claims.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: How to find middle ground when your partner wants kids — and you don't
item.description: Psychotherapist Merle Bombardieri has been helping couples with this conundrum for decades. She shares four exercises to bring clarity to the situation — and find a solution that minimizes regret.
item.content:encoded: Psychotherapist Merle Bombardieri has been helping couples with this conundrum for decades. She shares four exercises to bring clarity to the situation — and find a solution that minimizes regret.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: 4 surprising things to know about abortion in America since Dobbs
item.description: A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.
item.content:encoded:
. '/>

A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Why are crisis pregnancy centers saying they can 'rule out' ectopic pregnancy?
item.description: Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.
item.content:encoded:
Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for The Washington Post)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Inside the gold-mining town where the Ebola outbreak likely started
item.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: How to protect yourself from ticks
item.description: It's tick season, but how do you avoid the little arachnids? NPR asks two experts in tick bites and behavior.
item.content:encoded: It's tick season, but how do you avoid the little arachnids? NPR asks two experts in tick bites and behavior.
item.dc:creator: Hosts
